mcqs.question: Which method is called only once during the servlet lifecycle?
mcqs.explanation: The init() method is called only once when the servlet is first loaded. It is not called for each client request. The service() method is called for each request, and destroy() is called when the servlet is unloaded.

mcqs.question: Which HTTP method is used to request data from a specified resource?
mcqs.explanation: GET is used to request data from a specified resource. GET requests should have no side effects on the server (idempotent). POST is used to send data to create/update a resource, PUT to update, and DELETE to remove.

mcqs.question: In the Jakarta Servlet API, which interface does HttpServlet extend?
mcqs.explanation: HttpServlet extends GenericServlet, which implements the Servlet interface. GenericServlet provides a generic, protocol-independent implementation of the Servlet interface.

mcqs.question: What is the correct order of servlet lifecycle method execution?
mcqs.explanation: The servlet lifecycle is: 1) init() is called once during initialization, 2) service() is called for each HTTP request, 3) destroy() is called once when the servlet is unloaded from memory.

mcqs.question: Which method is used to read a request parameter in a servlet?
mcqs.explanation: The getParameter() method of HttpServletRequest is used to retrieve the value of a request parameter. It returns a String value or null if the parameter does not exist.

mcqs.question: Which JSP lifecycle method is equivalent to the servlet's service() method?
mcqs.explanation: The _jspService() method in JSP is equivalent to the servlet's service() method. It handles the actual request-response processing and is called for each request. Note that this method name starts with underscore and cannot be overridden.

mcqs.question: What is the default HTTP method when a form is submitted without specifying the method attribute?
mcqs.explanation: When the method attribute is not specified in an HTML form, it defaults to GET. GET is the default because it is simpler and suitable for retrieving data without causing side effects.

mcqs.question: In servlet programming, which method handles GET requests in HttpServlet?
mcqs.explanation: The doGet() method in HttpServlet is specifically designed to handle GET requests. By default, it returns an HTTP 405 (Method Not Allowed) error unless overridden.

mcqs.question: Which cookie method is used to set the maximum age of a cookie in seconds?
mcqs.explanation: The setMaxAge() method sets the maximum age of a cookie in seconds. A positive value indicates the cookie will persist for that many seconds. A value of 0 deletes the cookie, and a negative value makes it a session cookie.

mcqs.question: Which JSP tag is used to declare a method in a JSP page?
mcqs.explanation: The <%! %> declaration tag in JSP is used to declare methods and class-level variables. The content inside this tag is placed outside the _jspService() method during translation to a servlet.

mcqs.question: When does the servlet's destroy() method get called?

options.text: When the server shuts down or servlet is unloaded

mcqs.explanation: The destroy() method is called by the servlet container when the servlet is being taken out of service, typically during server shutdown or when the container decides to unload the servlet to free resources.

mcqs.question: Which method of HttpServletResponse is used to redirect a request to another URL?
mcqs.explanation: The sendRedirect() method of HttpServletResponse sends a temporary redirect response to the client with the new URL. The client's browser then makes a new request to the redirected URL (two requests total).

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the jspInit() method in JSP?
mcqs.explanation: The jspInit() method is called once when the JSP is first translated into a servlet and initialized. It is similar to the servlet's init() method and is used to initialize resources like database connections.

mcqs.question: Which method of HttpServletRequest returns all values of a parameter that has multiple values?
mcqs.explanation: The getParameterValues() method returns an array of String objects containing all the values of the specified parameter. This is useful for parameters that can have multiple values, like checkboxes or multiple-select dropdowns.

mcqs.question: In servlet lifecycle, which method receives the HTTP request and decides which handler method (doGet/doPost) to call?
mcqs.explanation: The service() method is the main entry point for handling requests. It receives the request and response objects, determines the HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.), and dispatches to the appropriate handler method like doGet() or doPost().

mcqs.question: Which JSP action tag is used to include another resource at request time?
mcqs.explanation: The <jsp:include> action tag includes another resource (JSP or HTML) at request time. The included content is processed and the result is merged with the current page. Compare with <%@ include %> which includes at translation time.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between forward() and sendRedirect()?

options.text: forward() is server-side, sendRedirect() is client-side

mcqs.explanation: forward() is a server-side redirect where the request is transferred to another resource on the same server without the client's knowledge (single request). sendRedirect() tells the client to make a new request to the new URL (two requests, can go to different servers).

mcqs.question: Which cookie method adds a cookie to the response?
mcqs.explanation: The addCookie() method of HttpServletResponse is used to add a cookie to the response that will be sent back to the client. The cookie is stored on the client's browser and sent with subsequent requests.

mcqs.question: In which phase of JSP lifecycle is the _jspService() method invoked?
mcqs.explanation: The _jspService() method is invoked during the request processing phase of the JSP lifecycle. It handles the actual HTTP request and generates the response content. This method is called for every client request.

mcqs.question: Which method would you override in HttpServlet to handle both GET and POST requests with the same logic?

options.text: Both A and B are correct

mcqs.explanation: You can override the service() method in HttpServlet to handle both GET and POST requests with the same logic. The service() method automatically dispatches to doGet() or doPost() based on the HTTP method. Alternatively, you can have both methods call a common helper method.
